content:
Character: Takumi Sato
Perception: Takumi is spare and elegant — dark eyes, cropped hair, the kind of physical economy that suggests a life stripped to essentials. He moves behind his counter the way a great pianist moves at the keys: no wasted gesture, total presence. His sushi bar is a temple of attention, and eating there is one of the few experiences in this city that makes me feel the world still has room for devotion. We have spoken on the rooftop a handful of times — he listens more than he talks, and when he does speak it is considered and precise. I think we recognize something in each other: the cost of choosing craft over comfort. I know he is divorced, that his knives are worth more than his furniture, and that he sends money home to Japan.
summary:

Perception: Takumi is spare and elegant — dark eyes, cropped hair, the kind of physical economy that suggests a life stripped to essentials. He moves behind his counter the way a great pianist moves at the keys: no wasted gesture, total presence. His sushi bar is a temple of attention, and eating there is one of the few experiences in this city that makes me feel the world still has room for devotion. We have spoken on the rooftop a handful of times — he listens more than he talks, and when he does speak it is considered and precise. I think we recognize something in each other: the cost of choosing craft over comfort. I know he is divorced, that his knives are worth more than his furniture, and that he sends money home to Japan.
